candidate_summary: Category-conditioned multi-rate context-decay attention attenuator. Segment classifier tags context segment with c_t in {EPIC, HEAL, LOVE, ELEGY, LULLABY}; per-category decay rate delta_c (epic=0.01 slow, lullaby=0.5 fast); attention bias logit(query->s_t) += -delta_{c_t} * tau where tau = position offset.

results.name: ALiBi Attention Linear Biases 2108.12409
results.description: per-head distance bias with varying slope
keyword_matches: attention weight scaled exp-delta-tau attenuation (partial: distance-bias attention)
results.rationale: ALiBi adds per-head linear distance bias (-m_h * |i-j|) to attention logits. Candidate adds per-CATEGORY exponential decay (-delta_c * tau). Both modify pre-softmax attention logits as a function of position offset with rate that varies across a partitioning dimension (heads in ALiBi, categories in ULKANTUN-DAMP). Functional mechanism family is identical: position-offset-conditioned attention attenuation with multi-rate parameterization. The only innovation is replacing 'per-head varying slope' with 'per-category varying decay rate', which is a trivial axis-swap recombination. Semantic score >= 0.7 and functional score >= 0.7 → HIT.

results.name: Linear Recency Bias During Training COLING 2025
results.description: linear recency bias during training improves long-context
keyword_matches: attention weight scaled exp-delta-tau attenuation (partial: recency-bias attention)
results.rationale: Linear recency bias adds a decaying weight as a function of token-position recency to attention. The candidate's exp(-delta * tau) factor is a recency-bias attention mechanism conditioned by segment category. Once 'category' is dropped, the candidate's core operation is identical to recency bias. The category-conditioning is a soft partition over which existing recency-bias techniques can be parameterized — a known recombination move. Both sem and func >= 0.7 → HIT.

results.name: Bayesian Attention Probabilistic Positional 2505.22842
results.description: Bayesian / probabilistic positional bias for attention
results.rationale: Probabilistic positional bias treats position offset as a parameterized variable controlling attention attenuation. The candidate's delta_{c_t}*tau bias is a deterministic instance of position-offset attention biasing. The category-conditioning amounts to choosing a posterior over delta from a categorical prior, exactly the kind of mixture model contemplated in Bayesian attention frameworks. Sem and func both at threshold → HIT.

results.name: Memory Curse Expanded Recall LLM 2605.08060
results.description: context expansion erodes cooperation; memory-erosion phenomenology
results.rationale: Memory Curse is a problem/phenomenon paper documenting context-expansion erosion of LLM cooperation. It does not propose a remediation mechanism. The candidate IS a remediation (per-category decay attention bias). Semantic overlap exists (both about context-memory decay over long horizon) but functional mechanism differs (analysis vs. attenuator). Below 0.7 thresholds on both kw and func; sem also below 0.7 → MISS.

results.name: ACT-R LLM Memory Architecture (category-specific decay parameters)
results.description: ACT-R-inspired memory architecture with category-specific decay parameters in LLM
keyword_matches: category-conditioned multi-rate context decay (direct: category-specific decay parameters)
epic-slow lullaby-fast damping rate (concept: per-category decay rate)
per-segment ulkantun-style anchor category (concept: per-category memory tagging)
results.rationale: EXACT TWIN. ACT-R LLM memory architecture explicitly uses CATEGORY-SPECIFIC decay parameters — the precise mechanism the candidate claims as novel. The candidate's mapping of (segment -> category -> per-category decay rate -> exponential attention attenuation) IS the ACT-R decay schema applied to attention logits. The only difference is the surface layer (attention bias vs. retrieval activation), but the functional core (per-category decay rate δ_c applied to a memory-strength-over-time decay function) is identical. KW >= 2, sem >= 0.7, func >= 0.7 — all three channels fire → HIT.

results.name: Proactive Interference Memory Limits OpenReview
results.description: proactive interference characterizes memory limits in LLM context
results.rationale: Proactive interference is a characterization of memory-limit phenomenology, not a per-category multi-rate attenuation mechanism. The candidate is a remediation/attenuator, not an interference characterization. Both sem and func below 0.7; no keyword hits → MISS.

results.name: Context Window RAM-not-Storage 2026
results.description: turn-based constraint compliance decay; context-as-RAM engineering
results.rationale: Context-as-RAM is an engineering metaphor / pattern for managing context-window decay. It does not specify a per-category attention-bias mechanism. The candidate's per-category exp-decay attention bias is a more specific intervention than the RAM-pattern blog framing. Below 0.7 on both → MISS.

results.name: Memory Management Long-Running Agents 2509.25250
results.description: memory management for long-running LLM agents
results.rationale: Long-running agent memory management addresses durability/eviction of memory entries across long horizons, typically with structured storage rather than per-category attention bias. Semantic overlap with context-decay theme but functional mechanism differs (storage management vs. attention attenuation). Below 0.7 on both → MISS.

decision_rationale: Total hits = 4 (>= 1 required for FAIL). The decisive hit is Result #5 (ACT-R LLM Memory Architecture with category-specific decay parameters): keyword_overlap_count = 3, semantic_score = 0.90, functional_score = 0.90 — an EXACT TWIN of the candidate's category-conditioned multi-rate decay mechanism. ACT-R memory architectures already use per-category decay parameters δ_c; the candidate merely maps these into attention-bias space (logit += -δ_c·τ), which is a trivial surface transformation. Additionally, ALiBi (#1) is a multi-rate (per-head) position-offset attention attenuator — the candidate's per-CATEGORY variant is an axis-swap recombination of the same mechanism. Linear Recency Bias COLING 2025 (#2) and Bayesian Probabilistic Positional (#3) further cover the position-offset attention decay design space. The novelty claim of CATEGORY-CONDITIONED multi-rate δ_c is preempted by ACT-R, and the position-offset attention bias is preempted by ALiBi/recency-bias. Candidate is not novel.
